sales.title:
ROMAN REPUBLIC. AR Quadrigatus (Didrachm) (6.58 gms), Rome Mint, ca. 225-212 B.C. NGC Ch AU, Strike: 5/5 Surface: 3/5. Brushed, Light Scratch.
Cr-28/3; Syd-64A; RSC-23. Obverse: Laureate head of Janus, with curved truncation; Reverse: Jupiter, hurling thunderbolt and holding scepter, in galloping quadriga driven right by Victory, holding reins in both hands; ROMA incuse on raised tablet in exergue. Fairly well centered and extremely lustrous, this type dazzles and without much in the way of handling at all.
sales.description: Stack’s Bowers and Ponterio | ['Manhattan Sale']

Cr-28/3; Syd-64A; RSC-23. Obverse: Laureate head of Janus
